title: Dog Coughing After Boarding: Causes and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Is your dog coughing after a kennel stay? Learn about Kennel Cough, urgency levels, and how to help your pet recover after boarding.
content_html:
TL;DR: A cough after boarding is usually Kennel Cough, which is highly contagious but often manageable with rest unless signs of pneumonia, such as labored breathing or lethargy, appear. Recording a video of the cough is essential for an accurate veterinary triage as dogs often mask symptoms in a clinical setting.

Why does my dog have a persistent cough after being at a boarding facility?

It is very common for dogs to develop a persistent, hacking cough after spending time in boarding facilities or doggy daycares. This is most often caused by Canine Infectious Respiratory Disease Complex (CIRDC), commonly known as Kennel Cough. This condition is highly contagious and spreads quickly through airborne droplets, shared water bowls, or direct contact in communal environments.

When is a post-boarding cough considered a veterinary emergency?

  • The urgency level for a post-boarding cough is typically Medium.
  • While most cases are similar to a human cold and resolve with rest, the condition can progress to more serious issues like pneumonia.
  • Contact a veterinarian promptly if your dog appears lethargic or loses their appetite.
  • Seek immediate care if your dog has difficulty breathing or if the cough is accompanied by thick yellow or green nasal discharge.

How does recording a video of my dog's cough help with the triage process?

  • Providing a video is a vital tool because dogs often stop coughing once they arrive at a clinic due to environmental stress or excitement.
  • A recording allows the vet to hear the specific sound of the cough—such as a dry "honk" or a moist rattle.
  • Vets can observe your dog's respiratory effort in a relaxed setting, which is essential for determining the severity of the illness.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Following boarding, a harsh, dry cough, potentially followed by retching or gagging, is a prominent clinical sign, easily induced by gentle palpation of the larynx or trachea. This syndrome, suspected when the cough develops 5-10 days after exposure to other dogs, typically diminishes in severity within the first 5 days but can persist for 10-20 days. While the roles of canine adenovirus 1 (CAV-1) and Mycoplasma sp are not clearly established, Bordetella bronchiseptica may act as a primary pathogen, especially in young dogs, and other bacteria (e.g., Pseudomonas sp, Escherichia coli, Klebsiella pneumoniae) may cause secondary infections. Thoracic radiographs are essential to determine the severity of disease and to exclude other causes of cough. Affected dogs often exhibit few additional clinical signs, except for partial anorexia, with body temperature and WBC counts typically remaining normal. The development of more severe signs, including fever, purulent nasal discharge, depression, anorexia, and a productive cough, especially in puppies, indicates a complicating systemic infection. Cough suppressants containing codeine derivatives, such as hydrocodone (0.25 mg/kg, PO, bid-qid) or butorphanol (0.05-0.1 mg/kg, PO or SC, bid-qid), should be used only as needed to control persistent nonproductive coughing. Antibiotics are usually not needed except in severe chronic cases.

Chapter: Infectious Disease, Respiratory

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1492)

faqs.question: How long does it take for kennel cough to show up after boarding?
faqs.answer: Symptoms typically appear 3 to 10 days after exposure to an infected dog or contaminated environment.

faqs.question: Can my dog get kennel cough if they were vaccinated?
faqs.answer: Yes. Similar to the human flu vaccine, the Bordetella vaccine protects against the most common strains but cannot prevent every single respiratory virus or bacteria.

faqs.question: Is kennel cough contagious to other pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is highly contagious to other dogs. It is best to keep your coughing dog isolated from other canines until they have been cleared by a vet.
